code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to clean numeric objects (e.g., remove trailing .0)
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        # Remove surrounding quotes only; keep original numeric formatting
        return o.strip('"')

    # More natural templates; some include the subject directly
    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The key is the predicate name; the value is a format string that can be
    # interpolated with the cleaned object via the placeholder {obj}.
    templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "city": "is located in the city of {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "runwayLength": "The runway length of {subject} is {obj}",
        "runwayName": "{obj} is the runway name of {subject}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is located in the country of {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "The ICAO Location Identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "The IATA location identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "The IATA location identifier of {subject} is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has a runway surface of {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "the length of the first runway is {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "the length of the first runway is {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "the first runway number is {obj}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the second runway is {obj}",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the third runway is {obj}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "the length of the fourth runway is {obj} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the fourth runway is {obj}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "the fifth runway number is {obj}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "the surface of the fifth runway is {obj}",
        # General predicates
        "language": "The {obj} are spoken in {subject}",
        "officialLanguage": "The official language of {subject} is {obj}",
        "headquarter": "The headquarters of {subject} are located in {obj}",
        "headquarters": "The headquarters of {subject} are located in {obj}",
        "leader": "The leader of {subject} is {obj}",
        "owner": "The owner of {subject} is {obj}",
        "capital": "The capital of {subject} is {obj}",
        # Entity classification predicates (class, order, division, etc.)
        "class": "{subject} class is {obj}",
        "order": "{subject} order is {obj}",
        "division": "{subject} division is {obj}",
        "family": "{subject} family is {obj}",
        # Fallback generic template
        "default": "{obj} is the {predicate} of {subject}"
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        # Collect objects by predicate for easy lookup
        data = {}
        for t in tlist:
            data[t.predicate] = _clean_obj(t.object)

        # Keep track of predicates that have been explicitly rendered
        handled = {
            "runwayName",
            "location",
            "cityServed",
            "city",
            "runwayLength",
            "1stRunwayLengthFeet",
            "1stRunwayLengthMetre",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres",
            "operatingOrganisation",
        }

        phrase_parts = []

        # Runway name (most salient)
        if "runwayName" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"the {data['runwayName']} runway")
        else:
            phrase_parts.append("the airport")

        # Location / city information
        if "location" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"in {data['location']}")
        elif "cityServed" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"serving {data['cityServed']}")
        elif "city" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"in {data['city']}")

        # Runway length
        if "runwayLength" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"has a length of {data['runwayLength']}")
        if "1stRunwayLengthFeet" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"first runway length {data['1stRunwayLengthFeet']} feet")
        if "1stRunwayLengthMetre" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"first runway length {data['1stRunwayLengthMetre']} metres")

        # Elevation
        if "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"and is situated {data['elevationAboveTheSeaLevel']} metres above sea level")
        if "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"and is situated {data['elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet']} feet above sea level")
        if "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"and is situated {data['elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres']} metres above sea level")

        # Operating organisation
        if "operatingOrganisation" in data:
            phrase_parts.append(f"operated by {data['operatingOrganisation']}")

        # Render any remaining predicates using templates or generic fallback
        for pred, obj in data.items():
            if pred in handled:
                continue
            tmpl = templates.get(pred)
            if tmpl:
                # Template may already contain the subject
                if "{subject}" in tmpl:
                    sentences.append(tmpl.format(subject=subject, obj=obj) + ".")
                else:
                    phrase_parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                generic = pred.replace('_', ' ')
                phrase_parts.append(f"{generic} {obj}")

        # Assemble the final sentence for this subject
        sentence = f"{subject} " + " ".join(phrase_parts) + "."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    # Join all generated sentences; if there is only one, return it without extra spaces
    return " ".join(sentences).strip()

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
